sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, ip_address INET, character_data VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, ip_address, character_data) VALUES (1, inet '192.168.1.1', 'test string'), (2, inet '192.168.1.2', 'another test€'), (3, inet '192.168.1.3', '€character string');
SELECT id, length(character_data) AS char_length FROM test_table WHERE ip_address <<= inet '192.168.1.0/24';
SELECT id, length(character_data) AS char_length FROM test_table WHERE length(character_data) > 10 AND ip_address <<= inet '192.168.1.3';
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY id) AS count_per_id FROM (SELECT id, length(character_data) AS char_length FROM test_table WHERE length(character_data) IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
SELECT a.id, length(a.character_data) AS char_length FROM test_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.ip_address <<= b.ip_address WHERE b.id = 2;
SELECT id, MAX(length(character_data)) AS max_length FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING MAX(length(character_data)) <= 255;
SELECT id, length(character_data), ip_address <<= inet '192.168.1.0/24' AS ip_check FROM test_table WHERE ip_check = true AND length(character_data) = 0;
SELECT id, length(character_data) AS char_length FROM test_table ORDER BY char_length DESC FETCH FIRST 2 ROWS ONLY;
